other_observers: Bill Day. I also received a report from Bob Moston saying that he saw what he believed to be a COBH later that day (~11:45) one mile east of Fruitgrowers Reservoir.; I spotted the bird and went to find Bill Day so that he could also see it immediately after I spotted it.
specific_location: Soaring above the town of Hotchkiss. I viewed the bird through binoculars from Hwy 92/4th St. intersection.
description: I first took notice of this raptor because it was dark (black) and had the distinctive shape of a buteo as I was driving. The date, June 12th, would be an unusual date for a dark-morph Red-tailed Hawk in Colorado. The very broad wings also caught my attention even before I raised my binoculars for closer inspection. Once I did stop my car and get my binoculars on this bird I again realized that this was not a Red-tailed Hawk because of its shape (very broad wings and the impression of a short tail) and it was entirely black except for a wide white band at the base of its tail. I know that a narrow white terminal band is supposed to be evident on Common Black-Hawks; however, I could not see any white at the end of the tail possibly because of the distance. At one point I did see this hawk soaring with a Red-tailed Hawk and noted that it seemed to be about the same size as the Red-tailed Hawk.Behaviors: This raptor was soaring the entire time I viewed it. I watched it go approximately one half mile from the middle of Hotchkiss to the west. I did not see it flap during the time I watched it.Call: no vocalizations heard.Plumage: adult
